data.title: State-Owned Cornerstone Investor Extends Lock-Up on Guoxia Technology H Shares
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The latest announcement is out from Guoxia Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2655) ).

Guoxia Technology Co., Ltd. announced that Huikai Hong Kong Economic Development Co., Ltd., a state-owned investment platform under Wuxi Huishan Economic Development Zone and a cornerstone investor in the company’s H-share IPO, has voluntarily extended the lock-up period on its stake. The investor will refrain from disposing of its 2,699,000 H shares, representing about 0.68% of H shares and 0.52% of total issued share capital, for 18 months starting from 16 June 2026.

The company said the move underscores strong confidence from a PRC state-owned cornerstone investor in its long-term value, AI-driven energy storage technology and expanding overseas business. While the locked-up stake is relatively small, the decision is seen as a positive signal for market sentiment and reinforces Guoxia’s positioning as a technology-focused energy storage player, though shareholders and potential investors are cautioned to trade the H shares carefully.

More about Guoxia Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Guoxia Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based company focused on energy storage solutions that integrate artificial intelligence, positioning its business in the fast-growing “AI+” energy storage segment. The company is expanding its overseas footprint, aiming to leverage its technological advantages to capture long-term growth opportunities in international energy and technology markets.

Average Trading Volume: 8,733,336

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$7.06B

An update from Hollwin Urban Operation Service Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2529) ) is now available.

Hollwin Urban Operation Service Group Co., Ltd. has convened an extraordinary shareholders’ meeting for June 30, 2026, in Changsha, Hunan Province, to seek investor approval on key corporate actions. The meeting will be open to eligible shareholders and their proxies, with voting conducted by poll in accordance with Hong Kong listing requirements.

Shareholders will vote on approving and ratifying a share acquisition agreement, authorizing the board to execute all related documents and implementation steps. They will also consider a proposed change in the use of proceeds, signaling potential adjustments in capital allocation that could affect the company’s future deployment of funds and overall strategic direction.

More about Hollwin Urban Operation Service Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Hollwin Urban Operation Service Group Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 02529. The group operates in the urban operation services sector, focusing on providing integrated operational and management services for urban environments and related projects in mainland China, with a corporate base in Changsha, Hunan Province.

Asiaray Media Group Ltd. ( (HK:1993) ) just unveiled an update.

Asiaray Media Group Limited reported that all resolutions at its 12 June 2026 annual general meeting were approved by shareholders via poll, including the adoption of a new memorandum and articles of association. The meeting also confirmed the receipt of the 2025 financial statements, re-election of key directors, reappointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and renewal of share issuance and repurchase mandates, underscoring strong shareholder support for the company’s governance framework and capital management flexibility.

The company announced the retirement of a director and corresponding changes to board committee composition, while simultaneously updating its constitutional documents to the new M&A. These moves indicate a broader corporate governance refresh that may enhance regulatory alignment and board oversight, with potential implications for how Asiaray structures future strategic decisions and responds to evolving listing requirements.

More about Asiaray Media Group Ltd.

Asiaray Media Group Limited is an out-of-home advertising company listed in Hong Kong, operating primarily in transit and outdoor media across Greater China and other Asian markets. The group focuses on integrated space management solutions, leveraging media resources in airports, metro systems and other high-traffic venues to reach urban consumers and brand advertisers.

data.title: Asiaray Reshapes Board Committees and Confirms Leadership Structure
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Asiaray Media Group Ltd. ( (HK:1993) ) has provided an update.

Asiaray Media Group Limited has announced an updated board composition effective 12 June 2026, confirming Vincent Lam Tak Hing as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer alongside executive director Kwan Tat Cheong and non-executive director Huang Hai. The board also comprises three independent non-executive directors, Ma Ho Fai, Mak Ka Ling and Wu Tak Lung, reflecting a balanced governance structure.

The company has reorganized its three key board committees, assigning Wu Tak Lung to chair the audit committee, Ma Ho Fai to lead the remuneration committee, and Vincent Lam to chair the nomination committee, with other directors serving as members. This allocation clarifies oversight roles in audit, pay, and board appointments, which may strengthen corporate governance and reassure investors about the company’s control and accountability framework.

More about Asiaray Media Group Ltd.

Asiaray Media Group Limited is an out-of-home advertising company listed in Hong Kong, focusing on media resources across transport, airport, and other urban locations in Asia. The group delivers integrated advertising solutions and operates under a board structure that includes executive, non-executive, and independent non-executive directors to oversee governance and strategic direction.

Asiaray Media Group Ltd. ( (HK:1993) ) has provided an update.

Asiaray Media Group Limited has appointed veteran accountant and corporate governance specialist Wu Tak Lung as an independent non-executive director, chairman of its audit committee and a member of its remuneration committee, effective immediately after the company’s 12 June 2026 annual general meeting. Wu brings extensive audit, finance and board experience from roles at Deloitte, multiple Hong Kong listed companies and public institutions, and will receive annual remuneration of HK$304,000, with his board tenure subject to shareholder re-election under the company’s governance rules.

The appointment strengthens Asiaray’s audit oversight and remuneration governance at a time when regulatory and investor scrutiny of board independence and ESG reporting is intensifying in Hong Kong. By adding a director with deep expertise in accounting, risk management and ESG certification, the company signals a focus on bolstering internal controls and board committees, which may reassure shareholders and other stakeholders about its compliance and governance standards.

More about Asiaray Media Group Ltd.

Asiaray Media Group Limited is an out-of-home media company based in Hong Kong, focusing on advertising and media services across transport and other public venues in Greater China and selected Asian markets. The group provides integrated media solutions to brands, leveraging transit, airport, and outdoor advertising formats to reach urban consumers.

Homeland Interactive Technology Ltd. ( (HK:3798) ) has shared an update.

Homeland Interactive Technology Ltd. reported that all resolutions at its 12 June 2026 annual general meeting in Hong Kong were approved by shareholders via poll, with 1,189,177,797 shares eligible to vote after excluding treasury shares and those held under its 2019 Share Award Scheme. The meeting confirmed the company’s 2025 financial statements, re-elected executive and independent non-executive directors, reappointed Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor, and renewed general mandates for share repurchases and new share issuance, reinforcing board continuity and capital management flexibility for future corporate actions.

Approval of mandates to repurchase up to 10% of issued share capital and issue up to 20% new shares, including potential sales of treasury shares, provides the company with tools to manage its balance sheet, reward employees and respond to financing needs, which may influence future dilution, buybacks and capital allocation decisions for investors.

More about Homeland Interactive Technology Ltd.

Homeland Interactive Technology Ltd. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the interactive technology and gaming sector. The group focuses on developing and operating online entertainment products, serving users primarily in mainland China and other selected markets, and relies on equity-based incentives such as share award schemes to support its talent and growth strategy.

data.title: Tianjin Construction Development Group Details HK$44 Million Share Subscription and Use of Proceeds
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The latest announcement is out from Tianjin Construction Development Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2515) ).

Tianjin Construction Development Group has detailed the investors in its latest share subscription under a general mandate, naming five individual subscribers and one corporate investor, Primary Winner Limited, whose ultimate beneficial owner is Chinese investor Zhang Jianchao. All subscribers are classified as independent third parties, and none will become substantial shareholders upon completion of the transaction, which involves 51.79 million new shares for total proceeds of about HK$44 million.

The company plans to allocate roughly 60% of the net proceeds to ongoing administrative and operating expenses, with around 30% earmarked for staff costs, 20% for daily operating items such as rent and professional fees, and 10% for liquidity support to bolster cash flow and operational stability. This capital raise is expected to reinforce the group’s short-term financial flexibility and support the continuity of its day-to-day construction-related operations without materially altering its shareholder structure.

More about Tianjin Construction Development Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Tianjin Construction Development Group Co., Ltd., listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2515, operates in the construction and infrastructure sector in the People’s Republic of China. The company focuses on development and related services, positioning itself to serve urban construction and associated projects within the mainland market and beyond.

Bank of Jiujiang Co., Ltd., a Chinese commercial bank with H shares listed in Hong Kong, focuses on providing banking services to corporate and individual clients while leveraging its cross-border capital market presence. The bank declared a final cash dividend of RMB 0.56 per 10 shares for the year ended 31 December 2025, with Hong Kong shareholders to receive the equivalent of HKD 0.643914 per 10 shares based on a specified exchange rate. data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The latest update is out from Strong Petrochemical Holdings Limited ( (HK:0852) ).

Strong Petrochemical Holdings Limited reported its audited consolidated results for the year ended 31 December 2024, releasing financial statements for the group and its subsidiaries alongside comparative figures for 2023. The announcement includes an extract of the independent auditor’s report, which provides an overview of the group’s financial position, performance and cash flows for the period.

The independent auditor issued a qualified opinion on the 2024 consolidated financial statements, stating they present a true and fair view except for matters outlined in the basis for qualified opinion. The qualification stems from a scope limitation related to Strong HK’s crude oil trading transactions with certain third parties, indicating that some aspects of these transactions could not be fully verified and may affect stakeholders’ assessment of the group’s trading operations and risk profile.

More about Strong Petrochemical Holdings Limited

Strong Petrochemical Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating in the petrochemical sector. Through its subsidiaries, including Strong Petrochemical Limited (Strong HK), the group is involved in trading crude oil and related petrochemical products, targeting international commodities and energy markets.

data.description:

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Tycoon Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:3390) ) just unveiled an update.

Tycoon Group Holdings is revising the annual caps for certain continuing connected transactions with JBM Group after reviewing distribution agreements and concluding that transaction volumes are likely to exceed previous projections. The terms of the relevant agreements remain unchanged, and other distribution agreements, including those covering Europharm and Jacobson Medical products, keep their existing caps and conditions.

The company has also entered into a new Foci Distribution Agreement appointing its subsidiary Fu Qing as the exclusive distributor of Foci products in Singapore, formalizing a connected transaction due to overlapping shareholding with JBM. In addition, Tycoon Global has agreed to increase the royalty rate payable to JMM under a Trademark Licence Agreement from 20% to 30% of net selling price and to revise annual caps, reflecting expectations of higher sales of products bearing the licensed trademarks and signaling growth in licensed-brand volumes within its portfolio.

More about Tycoon Group Holdings Limited

Tycoon Group Holdings Limited is a healthcare and wellness products distributor headquartered in Hong Kong, with operations involving branded pharmaceutical and consumer health products. data.title: Yuk Wing to Acquire 65% Stake in Anhui Power Technology Firm, Adds Call Option for Further Expansion
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1536) ) just unveiled an update.

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. has agreed, through a wholly owned subsidiary, to acquire a 65% equity interest in Anhui Runsheng Electric Power Technology Co., Ltd. for RMB37.5 million, with the target to become an indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary whose financial results will be consolidated into the group’s accounts. The deal is classified as a major transaction under Hong Kong listing rules and will be put to shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting, alongside approval for a call option that allows Yuk Wing to buy additional equity within 12 months, signaling a potential deepening of its stake and future expansion of the enlarged group.

Under the sale and purchase agreement, the seller will grant Yuk Wing a call option over further shares in the target at a nominal upfront consideration, with a total exercise price of RMB20.1 million if fully used. The company plans to send a circular with financial details of the target and pro forma information on the enlarged group by early July 2026, as it seeks to strengthen its position in the power technology segment and provide greater clarity to investors on the acquisition’s financial impact.

More about Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd.

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company that expands its portfolio through strategic acquisitions and investments. The group operates via wholly owned subsidiaries and focuses on consolidating newly acquired businesses into its existing operations to drive growth and enhance shareholder value.

data.title: China Resources Land Growth in Sales Value and Rental Income for May 2026
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

China Resources Land ( (HK:1109) ) has shared an update.

China Resources Land reported that in May 2026 it achieved gross contracted sales of about RMB23.51 billion, a 28.1% year-on-year increase, even as contracted gross floor area fell 19.7% to 0.521 million square metres. For the first five months of 2026, sales reached RMB93.51 billion, up 7.7% year-on-year, with contracted GFA down 26.6%, suggesting higher average selling prices or a shift in project mix amid a softer volume environment.

The group’s recurring revenue in May rose 8.6% year-on-year to RMB4.44 billion, driven by an 11.4% rise in rental income to RMB3.00 billion from its investment properties. Cumulative recurring revenue for the first five months climbed 7.7% to RMB22.01 billion, with rental income up 12.9% to RMB15.00 billion, underscoring the growing contribution of stable rental streams to the company’s earnings profile despite volatility in contracted sales volumes.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1109) stock is a Buy
with a HK$34.90 price target.

see the HK:1109 Stock Forecast page.

China Resources Land Limited is a mainland China-focused real estate developer and property operator listed in Hong Kong, engaged in residential development and investment property rental businesses. The group generates revenue from contracted property sales as well as recurring income from a sizable portfolio of investment properties, positioning it as a major player in China’s urban commercial and residential real estate markets.

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3759) ) has issued an update.

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. reported the results of its 2025 annual general meeting in Beijing, confirming that the meeting was validly convened under PRC law and its Articles of Association, with about 48.7% of voting shares represented. All directors attended, and independent scrutineers, including the H share registrar and the company’s PRC legal adviser, supervised the poll process in line with Hong Kong listing requirements.

Shareholders approved all ordinary resolutions, including the 2025 profit distribution and 2026 interim dividend plan, the 2025 annual report and results, director remuneration and a new remuneration policy, an estimated hedging quota for 2026, and the appointment of domestic and international auditors. The meeting also endorsed the election of a non-executive director to the fourth board and amendments to the Articles of Association, reinforcing Pharmaron’s governance framework and capital management plans for the coming year.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3759) stock is a Buy
with a HK$28.10 price target.

More about Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. Class H

Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd. is a China-based contract research and development services provider to the pharmaceutical and biotech industries, listed in Hong Kong with both A shares and H shares. The company focuses on offering integrated R&D and related services to global drug developers, positioning itself as a key player in the life sciences outsourcing market.

data.description:

Hengxin Technology Ltd. ( (HK:1085) ) has shared an update.

Hengxin Technology’s subsidiary Qinghai Zhongkong has signed three major contracts with companies from GCL Group for the Delingha 350 MW solar thermal project in Qinghai, with a total value of about RMB4.73 billion. The agreements cover construction works by GCL Green Energy, supply of key equipment by Lvyuan Xinneng, and installation and commissioning services by GCL Green Energy, marking a significant expansion of Hengxin’s exposure to large-scale solar thermal infrastructure in China.

Because the aggregated transaction size exceeds Hong Kong listing thresholds, the deals are classified as a very substantial acquisition and will require shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting. The company plans to issue a circular with further details by early July 2026, and has cautioned shareholders and potential investors that the transactions may not proceed if approval is not obtained, underscoring governance requirements and execution risk around this large-scale investment.

More about Hengxin Technology Ltd.

Hengxin Technology Ltd., incorporated in Singapore and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries such as Qinghai Zhongkong and is expanding into large-scale renewable energy infrastructure. The company is pursuing growth in utility-scale solar thermal projects in mainland China, positioning itself within the broader clean energy value chain.
